title: Ensemble Retrieval in Practice
author: samira
text: Ensemble retrieval combines BM25 keyword search with semantic vector search so the system can catch both exact terms and broader meaning.

title: Ensemble Retrieval in Practice
author: samira
text: Weighted fusion often works better than choosing a single retriever because one method covers the weaknesses of the other.

title: MMR for Diverse Context
author: nora
text: MMR reduces redundancy by rewarding relevance while penalizing documents that are too similar to already selected results.

title: MMR for Diverse Context
author: nora
text: Diversity-aware retrieval is useful when top-k results repeat the same point and waste the context window.

title: Parent Child Retrieval Guide
author: omar
text: Parent-child retrieval embeds smaller child chunks but returns a larger parent section so the generator sees richer context.

title: Parent Child Retrieval Guide
author: omar
text: This pattern gives precise retrieval during search while keeping enough surrounding detail for accurate answering.

title: Self Query Filters
author: nora
text: Self-query retrieval separates semantic intent from filters such as year author level and category.

title: Self Query Filters
author: nora
text: For example a user might ask for advanced Nora posts from 2025 about filters or retrieval and the system should parse both meaning and filters.

title: Reranking for Precision
author: liam
text: Reranking scores query document pairs jointly after broad first-stage retrieval and usually improves final precision.

title: Reranking for Precision
author: liam
text: A common production pattern is fetch ten results quickly then rerank down to the best three for higher precision.

title: BM25 and Exact Match
author: samira
text: BM25 remains strong for exact term matching acronyms error codes and product names that vector search may blur.

title: BM25 and Exact Match
author: samira
text: Keyword retrieval is especially useful when users type exact phrases that appear in technical documents.

title: Retrieval Bottlenecks
author: omar
text: If retrieval recall is weak the generator never sees the right evidence so the final answer quality stays low.

title: Retrieval Bottlenecks
author: omar
text: Fix retrieval before tuning generation prompts or changing the LLM because generation cannot rescue missing context.
